addLiquidityProportionalToERC4626Pool(ad.details: An "ERC4626 pool" contains IERC4626 yield-bearing tokens (e.g., waDAI). Ensure that any buffers associated with the wrapped tokens in the ERC4626 pool have been initialized before initializing or adding liquidity to the "parent" pool, and also make sure limits are set properly.

params.exactBptAmountOut: Exact amount of pool tokens to be received
params.maxAmountsIn: Maximum amounts of underlying/wrapped tokens in, sorted in token registration order
params.userData: Additional (optional) data required for adding liquidity
params.wethIsEth: If true, incoming ETH will be wrapped to WETH and outgoing WETH will be unwrapped to ETH
params.wrapUnderlying: Flags indicating whether the corresponding token should be wrapped or used as an ERC20

returns._0: Actual amounts of tokens added to the pool

addLiquidityUnbalancedNestedPool(address.details: A nested pool is one in which one or more tokens are BPTs from another pool (child pool). Since there are multiple pools involved, the token order is not well-defined, and must be specified by the caller. If the parent or nested pools contain ERC4626 tokens that appear in the `tokensToWrap` list, they will be wrapped and their underlying tokens pulled as input, and expected to appear in `tokensIn`. Otherwise, they will be treated as regular tokens. NB: Pools with "overlapping" tokens (i.e., both the parent and a child pool contain one or more of the tokens in `tokensIn`), are not supported! The gas cost to explicitly detect this rare edge case would be prohibitive, so behavior in this case is undefined.

params.exactAmountsIn: An array with the amountIn of each token, sorted in the same order as tokensIn
params.minBptAmountOut: Expected minimum amount of parent pool tokens to receive
params.parentPool: The address of the parent pool (which contains BPTs of other pools)
params.tokensIn: An array with all tokens from the child pools, and all non-BPT parent tokens, in arbitrary order
params.tokensToWrap: A list of ERC4626 tokens which should be wrapped if encountered during pool traversal
params.userData: Additional (optional) data required for the operation
params.wethIsEth: If true, incoming ETH will be wrapped to WETH and outgoing WETH will be unwrapped to ETH

returns._0: The actual amount of parent pool tokens received

Ensure that any buffers associated with the wrapped tokens in the ERC4626 pool have been initialized before initializing or adding liquidity to the "parent" pool, and also make sure limits are set properly.

params.exactAmountsIn: Exact amounts of underlying/wrapped tokens in, sorted in token registration order
params.minBptAmountOut: Minimum amount of pool tokens to be received
params.userData: Additional (optional) data required for adding liquidity
params.wethIsEth: If true, incoming ETH will be wrapped to WETH and outgoing WETH will be unwrapped to ETH

returns.bptAmountOut: Actual amount of pool tokens received

params.data: Encoded function calls to be executed in the batch.

removeLiquidityProportionalNestedPool(ad.details: A nested pool is one in which one or more tokens are BPTs from another pool (child pool). Since there are multiple pools involved, the token order is not well-defined, and must be specified by the caller. params.minAmountsOut: An array with the minimum amountOut of each token, sorted in the same order as tokensOut
params.parentPool: The address of the parent pool (which contains BPTs of other pools)
params.tokensOut: An array with all tokens from the child pools, and all non-BPT parent tokens, in arbitrary order
params.tokensToUnwrap: A list of ERC4626 tokens which should be unwrapped if encountered during pool traversal
params.userData: Additional (optional) data required for the operation
params.wethIsEth: If true, incoming ETH will be wrapped to WETH and outgoing WETH will be unwrapped to ETH

returns.amountsOut: An array with the actual amountOut of each token, sorted in the same order as tokensOut
